contents:
# South Korea Defense Ministry denies USFK troop reduction discussions after Trump's Germany remarks

## Overview
South Korea's Ministry of National Defense stated no talks remain underway with the US on reducing the 28,500 US Forces Korea (USFK) troops. Spokesperson Chung Binna emphasized USFK's role in deterring North Korean aggression during a regular briefing. The denial addressed speculation from US President Donald Trump's comments on potential troop cuts in Germany.[1][2][4][5]

## Details
Chung Binna said, "The primary mission of U.S. Forces Korea remains to maintain a robust combined defense posture with the South Korean military to deter and respond to North Korean aggression." She added that Seoul and Washington will continue consultations to ensure USFK's stable presence and strengthen joint defense. Trump's remarks came on April 29 local time, hinting at Germany reductions and a decision soon. Defense experts like Lee Sang-kyu from Korea Institute for Defense Analyses assessed no impact on USFK due to no changes in operational plans. Analyst Kim noted USFK's criticality in US strategy against China along the first island chain. The ministry sought to reassure the public amid growing speculation.[1][5]

**Location**: Seoul, South Korea

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @TheKorea_Times, which covers Korea defense, North Korea, US-ROK alliance, security policy. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Korea defense will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
